points.speaker: Ashley Bittle
points.text: No oral communications from the public were received for this meeting, as no viewers were present when the agenda item was called.
points.source: Ashley Bittle : Oral communications from the public, I don't see anyone viewing. So we have none at this moment.

points.speaker: Deanne Hui
points.text: The meeting was adjourned at 2:39 PM by Chair Deanne Hui after concluding agenda items, updates, and announcements.
points.source: Deanne Hui : ... so the meeting is now ending at 2:39.

points.speaker: Vicki Gilfix
points.text: Vicki nominated Deanne for chair position for 2023 calendar year.
points.source: Vicki Gilfix : I nominate DM. For chair

points.speaker: Elna Tymes
points.text: Elna volunteered for vice chair position for 2023 calendar year.
points.source: Elna Tymes : I'll volunteer to be VICE sure

points.speaker: Vicki Gilfix
points.text: Vicki Gilfix reported minimal recent activity for the Senior Center subcommittee and noted that subcommittee membership will change for the coming year, acknowledging the group had not advanced work during the previous period.
points.source: Vicki Gilfix : ... we ... do get to change our committee this time around for this coming year, we haven't. I haven't done much with the Senior Center subcommittee at all.

points.speaker: Elna Tymes
points.text: Elna Tymes noted transportation is a frequent concern across city groups. While door-to-door access is not guaranteed, city transport providers adjust routes to reach seniors. She suggested the committee annually report where seniors are concentrated, potentially via a State of Mountain View Seniors report.
points.source: Elna Tymes : ... transportation is topic that shows up ... they ... make every effort to reach ... behind some of the Google route ... Jim, that's our responsibility ... we might be something that we do on an annual basis ... State of Mountain View Seniors report ... we should be considering.

points.speaker: Jim Nichols
points.text: Jim expressed concern about transportation organizations staying current with changing senior population demographics and neighborhood aging patterns.
points.source: Jim Nichols : My my concern is that these organizations in the city are are keeping abreast of the movements of the seniors

points.speaker: Elna Tymes
points.text: Mountain View provides better senior transportation access than San Jose.
points.source: Elna Tymes : In fact, we do our job of that than San Jose does
